care.summary: A vibrant, easy-care philodendron with heart-shaped leaves splashed in lime-green and dark green variegation.
care.light: Bright indirect light for best variegation; tolerates medium light but patterns fade
care.water: Water when top inch of soil is dry; avoid keeping soil soggy
care.soil: Well-draining standard potting mix with perlite or orchid bark
care.humidity: Average to high; tolerates normal indoor humidity well
care.fertilizer: Balanced liquid fertilizer monthly spring through fall
care.toxicity: Toxic if ingested; keep away from pets and children
common_issues: Reverting to all-green leaves in low light conditions
Leggy growth with sparse leaves from insufficient light
Brown leaf tips from dry air or inconsistent watering
tips: Provide a moss pole or trellis for climbing — leaves grow larger when vertical
Prune long vines to encourage fuller growth
Easy to propagate from stem cuttings in water or soil
Rotate regularly for even variegation on all sides
